description: Implements the 5 customer-feedback initiatives from the 2026-04-29 review with Shane and Alex. Three migrations already shipped on 2026-05-01 (Initiatives 2/3/4). This PRD adds: (1) LinkedIn-style Home Feed as new 6th tab + default landing, (2) Two-Path Promotions UI consuming the May-1 schema (admin externals + tenant-paid via Stripe Checkout), (3) thin TypeScript types for V2 sales-pipeline scaffold (no UI), (4) auto-grouped Nearby feed by N-mile radius from property lat/lon, (5) Delta-app-inspired UI lightening — targeted dark-on-dark contrast fixes PLUS new lighter card-surface tokens (brand-cloud / brand-mist / brand-paper / brand-ink / brand-ink-muted) added to tailwind.config.js. Brand primary stays brand-navy for headers and tab bar background. Full PRD with verbatim source content lives at tasks/prd-engagement-ui-enhancement.md (root of project, not unit/). USER DIRECTIVE: do NOT break the app or UI; meticulously double-check every story. Every story includes npx tsc --noEmit + npm run brand-lint + iOS-simulator visual check as acceptance.

sourceDocuments.purpose: Verbatim customer transcript (2026-04-29 meeting with Shane and Alex). Full text reproduced as Appendix A in the markdown PRD.
Delta app reference — Find My Trip screen (light gray screen background, white card, navy header).
Delta app reference — Travel Information list (white card with chevron rows, navy header, light bottom nav).
Delta app reference — Book screen (white card with form inside, red CTA).
Delta app reference — Home/Explore (promotional cards on light surface).

Migration: Initiative 3 — Sales Pipeline DB Prep (V2 scaffolding). Adds promotion_leads table + 6-stage lifecycle enum (hot_lead, contacted, pending_approval, pre_production, approved, published). RLS admin-only. NO UI in this phase per migration comment — table is intentionally unused at rest until V2.

Migration: Initiative 4 — Property Clustering. Adds latitude/longitude to properties (nullable, range-checked) + property_groups + property_group_members many-to-many. Tenants can READ groups for their own properties; only admins mutate. THIS PRD chooses radius-based auto-grouping per user direction; property_groups stays in place for future labeled clusters but is not consumed here.

Migration: Initiative 2 — Two-Path Promotions. advertiser_id nullable; new columns created_by_admin_id, external_contact_name/email/phone. CHECK promotions_attribution_required ensures every row has either advertiser_id OR business_name. Existing RLS already permits admin authoring of admin-authored externals (advertiser_id IS NULL).

userDirectives: Make sure you don't break the app or the UI. Meticulously double-check your work.
Both tenants AND outside businesses can advertise. Everyone still needs admin approval. Admin can charge tenants and set prices either in the app or admin portal.
All properties (and the businesses/tenants inside them) are auto-grouped by distance from their address. No admin curation required for the radius behavior.
Add a 6th 'Home' tab as the new default landing — bottom nav becomes 6 tabs (Home, Directory, Promotions, Community, Alerts, Profile).

UI direction is Delta-app inspired: keep the navy primary brand for headers, but add lighter card surfaces (white card on light gray screen). Targeted contrast fixes everywhere else; no full re-theme of existing screens in this milestone.
preflightChecks: Read unit/CLAUDE.md before any UI work — brand contract is strict (4 fonts, 4 sizes, 4/8 spacing grid, ≥44pt tap, lucide icons only, brand-lint must pass).
Read unit/scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md before each iteration for the agent loop contract.
Read progress.txt 'Codebase Patterns' section at the top before starting (create the file with that section if missing).
Verify branch is ralph/engagement-ui-enhancement off main; create from main if missing.

qualityGates.e2e: cd unit && npm run maestro test maestro/flows/qa-04-full-sweep.yaml
qualityGates.visualCheck: iOS simulator (iPhone 16 Pro Max). Confirm zero regression on every existing screen for any UI story.

codebaseRealityNotes.purpose: Ground-truth facts verified during deep PRD review on 2026-05-02. Read these BEFORE implementing any story to avoid making the same wrong assumptions the first PRD draft made.
facts: There is NO `npm run typecheck` script. Use `npx tsc --noEmit` (tsc is at unit/node_modules/.bin/tsc).

promotions.payment_status enum: 'unpaid' | 'paid' | 'repayment_required' | 'refunded'. There is NO 'pending' or 'failed' value. Initial state for tenant promotions is 'unpaid'; portal webhook flips to 'paid'.

promotions.review_status enum: 'draft' | 'pending' | 'approved' | 'revision_requested' | 'rejected' | 'expired' | 'suspended'. New tenant promotions start at 'draft'; webhook flips to 'pending' on payment.
No stripe_session_id column on promotions. Session id lives on promotion_payment_attempts.stripe_checkout_session_id. Intent id lives on promotions.current_payment_intent_id.

Portal webhook already handles checkout.session.completed for promotions rows via session.metadata.promotionId. Mobile-tenant checkouts reuse it — no separate webhook Edge Function needed (US-013 was rewritten to reflect this). Path: portal/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts.
promotion_status_events is the audit table. Every state transition MUST insert a row: { promotion_id, from_*, to_*, actor_user_id, actor_type: 'admin'|'advertiser'|'webhook', note }.
stripe_webhook_events table provides idempotency — webhook inserts event.id; duplicate insert means already processed.
Post-login redirect lives at unit/app/_layout.tsx lines 75-92 (NOT lib/AuthContext.tsx). Line 91 currently routes to /(tabs)/directory.

brand-lint is an inline bash regex chain in unit/package.json scripts.brand-lint — NOT a separate file. It's a DENY-list. New brand-cloud/brand-mist/etc tokens don't match any banned pattern, so they pass without script changes.
promotions table was renamed from advertiser_promotions in 20260412000001_m4_monetization.sql. Use `promotions` everywhere.
is_landlord() and landlord_property_ids() helpers exist (migration 20260406000400_landlord_auth.sql).
profiles.property_ids is uuid[]. Use `ANY(p.property_ids)` for filtering — matches existing RLS patterns.
advertiser_profiles.stripe_customer_id exists; profiles.stripe_customer_id does NOT. Mobile tenant checkouts pass customer_email to Stripe instead of an existing customer id.
Edge functions use top-level Deno.serve and import from https://esm.sh. See supabase/functions/expire-promotions/index.ts for the canonical pattern. No deno.json. No `deno check` command in workflow.
unit/app/(admin)/promotions/ contains ONLY [id].tsx — no list/index file today. The admin promotions list is rendered inside (admin)/index.tsx via the useAdminPromotions hook.

Card component props: { children, onPress?, className? }. Default classes: bg-brand-navy-light rounded-xl shadow-sm border border-brand-blue/40. Adding a `variant` prop must default to current behavior. Path: unit/components/ui/Card.tsx.
expo-web-browser is NOT installed. expo-linking IS. Add expo-web-browser in US-014.

All referenced UI components exist at unit/components/ui/: Avatar, Badge, Button, Card, CategoryChips, EmptyState, FAB, GradientHeader, Input, LoadingScreen, Modal, SearchBar, SegmentedControl, StatusBadge.
Maestro path is unit/maestro/flows/ (NOT .maestro/). Runner needs JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21 (already in npm scripts).
Postgres cube and earthdistance extensions are NOT enabled yet. US-004 RPC migration must `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS cube; CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS earthdistance;` at top.

Existing 10 edge functions: add-property-to-admin, complete-onboarding, expire-promotions, invite-tenant, issue-refund, mark-escalated-requests, mark-overdue-invoices, send-escalation-email, send-invoice-email, send-push-notification.
Portal Stripe webhook env var is STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET (not STRIPE_PROMOTION_WEBHOOK_SECRET — that name was invented in the first draft and has been removed).

userStories.title: Add light-surface brand tokens to tailwind.config.js
userStories.description: As a developer, I need new lighter brand surface tokens (brand-cloud, brand-mist, brand-paper, brand-ink, brand-ink-muted) so subsequent UI stories have a vetted palette. Purely additive; must not change any existing on-screen pixel.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to theme.extend.colors.brand in unit/tailwind.config.js: cloud:'#F4F5F7', mist:'#FFFFFF', paper:'#E5E7EB', ink:'#101B29', 'ink-muted':'#465A75'
No existing class is removed or renamed

Update unit/CLAUDE.md Brand Theming section to document the new tokens and the rule: light tokens permitted only on Home Feed and explicitly migrated screens; header surfaces and tab bar background stay brand-navy
cd unit && npx tsc --noEmit passes
cd unit && npm run brand-lint passes (after US-016 updates lint to permit new tokens — both stories may need to ship together)
Open iOS simulator and confirm zero visual diff on every existing tab screen
userStories.notes: Foundation story. Purely additive Tailwind config + CLAUDE.md doc update. No JSX touched. Implemented 2026-05-02. brand-lint deny-list does not match the new tokens, so it passes without script changes; US-016 will add the additive surface-pairing rules.

userStories.title: Generate TypeScript types from May-1 migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TypeScript types reflecting the new columns/tables added by the three 2026-05-01 migrations so service code can use them without 'any'.
acceptanceCriteria: Add PromotionLead, PromotionLeadStage types in unit/services/promotions.ts (or new unit/services/promotionLeads.ts)
Add PropertyGroup, PropertyGroupMember types in unit/services/properties.ts (or new unit/services/propertyGroups.ts)
Extend existing Promotion type to include nullable created_by_admin_id, external_contact_name, external_contact_email, external_contact_phone; advertiser_id becomes nullable
Add derived discriminator kind: 'tenant' | 'external' on Promotion (computed from advertiser_id == null)
Extend existing Property type with latitude: number | null, longitude: number | null
PromotionLeadStage union mirrors SQL enum exactly: 'hot_lead' | 'contacted' | 'pending_approval' | 'pre_production' | 'approved' | 'published'
cd unit && npx tsc --noEmit passes
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-03. Promotion type extended with created_by_admin_id + external_contact_{name,email,phone}; PromotionKind union + getPromotionKind() helper exported (kept off the row type to avoid polluting Supabase select shape). Property type gained latitude/longitude (nullable); PROPERTY_COLUMNS updated. New files services/promotionLeads.ts and services/propertyGroups.ts hold V2-scaffold types. Caller in app/(admin)/properties.tsx now passes lat/lon: null on create. tsc + brand-lint pass.

userStories.title: Add geocoding helper + admin backfill script for property lat/lon
userStories.description: As an admin, I need every property to have latitude/longitude populated so the radius-based Nearby feed can compute distances. Existing properties are NULL after the May-1 migration.
acceptanceCriteria: Add unit/services/geocoding.ts exporting geocodeAddress(address: string): Promise<{lat: number, lon: number} | null> using OSM Nominatim with 1 req/sec rate limit and User-Agent: UNIT-app header
Add unit/scripts/backfill-property-coordinates.ts (run via tsx) — selects properties with NULL lat/lon, geocodes each address, updates the row. Idempotent.
Add updatePropertyCoordinates(propertyId, lat, lon) mutation in unit/services/properties.ts
On admin property create/edit, geocode automatically; failure non-blocking (NULL coords + console warn)
cd unit && npx tsc --noEmit passes
Run backfill script against dev Supabase; confirm every existing property has non-NULL coords
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-03. Added unit/services/geocoding.ts (Nominatim + 1 req/sec throttle + UNIT-app User-Agent; never throws, returns null on any failure). Added propertiesService.updateCoordinates() and standalone updatePropertyCoordinates() helper in unit/services/properties.ts. Wired geocoding into unit/app/(admin)/properties.tsx admin create flow — best-effort geocode using `${address}, ${city}, ${state}`, non-blocking on failure. Added unit/scripts/backfill-property-coordinates.ts (run via tsx) — selects rows where lat OR lon is NULL, uses SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY to bypass RLS, idempotent. Backfill script not auto-run against dev (requires service role key + network). tsc + brand-lint both green.

userStories.title: Add radius-based 'nearby properties' service
userStories.description: As a tenant, I want the app to know which properties are within ~2 miles of mine so the home feed can show a Nearby view. Customer doc explicitly chose auto-grouping by radius. The May-1 property_groups table stays for future labeled clusters but is NOT used here.
acceptanceCriteria: Add unit/services/nearbyProperties.ts exporting getNearbyPropertyIds(originPropertyId, radiusMiles=2): Promise<string[]>
Implementation choice: (a) supabase.rpc('nearby_property_ids', ...) using earthdistance/cube extensions, OR (b) client-side haversine. Document choice in file header.
If (a): add migration 20260502000001_nearby_property_ids_rpc.sql creating SECURITY DEFINER function returning setof uuid; CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS cube + earthdistance
Returns origin property as element 0 of the array
Add hook unit/hooks/useNearbyPropertyIds.ts wrapping React Query with key ['nearby-properties', originPropertyId, radiusMiles] and staleTime 1 hour
Unit test the haversine math (or RPC) against three known cases including the Daytona cluster
cd unit && npx tsc --noEmit and npm test pass
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-03. Chose client-side haversine over the SECURITY DEFINER RPC option — no migration, no PG extensions to provision, and the math is unit-testable as a pure function. Public API of getNearbyPropertyIds is stable, so a future swap to supabase.rpc() is a drop-in change. Added unit/services/nearbyProperties.ts (exports haversineMiles + getNearbyPropertyIds), unit/hooks/useNearbyPropertyIds.ts (React Query, key ['nearby-properties', originId, radiusMiles], staleTime 1h, disabled when originPropertyId is falsy), and __tests__/services/nearbyProperties.test.ts (4 haversine cases including the Daytona cluster + 3 service-level cases for null-coord origin, null-coord neighbours, and distance sorting). Origin is always element 0 even when its lat/lon are null. tsc + jest (12 suites / 59 tests) + brand-lint all green.

userStories.title: Add 'Home' tab as new default landing (6th tab, first position)
userStories.description: As a tenant, I want to land on a Home tab when I open the app instead of the Directory, so I see the day's activity at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Create unit/app/(tabs)/home.tsx as new tab (placeholder content; real feed lands in US-007)
Update unit/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx — register 'home' as FIRST <Tabs.Screen> with title 'Home' and Home icon from lucide-react-native. Order: Home, Directory, Promotions, Community, Alerts, Profile
Hide Home tab from admins via href: isAdmin ? null : undefined

Update post-login redirect at unit/app/_layout.tsx lines 75-92 (NOT lib/AuthContext.tsx — verified during PRD research) so authenticated tenants land on /(tabs)/home instead of /(tabs)/directory on line 91. Admins still land on (admin).
Placeholder uses font-lora-semibold text-3xl text-brand-gray on bg-brand-navy (passes brand-lint)
cd unit && npx tsc --noEmit passes
cd unit && npm run brand-lint passes
Maestro: tenant logs in → Home tab is selected by default; existing 5 tabs still navigate; Alerts badge still works
Verify in iOS simulator using dev-browser/Maestro

userStories.title: Activity feed service — unified stream of recent property events
userStories.description: As a tenant, I want a single chronological stream of activity (new promotions, posts, alerts, business sign-ups, landlord announcements) so I don't have to check 4 tabs to know what's new.
acceptanceCriteria: Add unit/services/activityFeed.ts exporting getActivityFeed(propertyIds: string[], limit=50): Promise<ActivityFeedItem[]>
ActivityFeedItem discriminated union: { kind: 'promotion'|'post'|'alert'|'tenant_signup'|'business_update'|'announcement', id, propertyId, occurredAt, title, subtitle, imageUrl?, ctaRoute? }
Implementation: parallel-fetch from existing services (promotions, posts, notifications, businesses filtered by created_at > NOW() - 30d), normalize, merge by occurredAt DESC
Accepts propertyIds: string[] so same service powers My Property (one id) and Nearby (origin + radius result)
Add hook unit/hooks/useActivityFeed.ts. Cache key ['activityFeed', propertyIds.join(','), limit]. staleTime 60s
No N+1 queries — each underlying source fetched exactly once
cd unit && npx tsc --noEmit and npm test pass

userStories.title: Build Home Feed screen (Delta-style cards on light surface)
userStories.description: As a tenant, I want a scrollable feed of activity cards on the Home tab — visually echoing Delta's white cards on a light gray background — so I can scan recent property activity quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace placeholder in unit/app/(tabs)/home.tsx with real screen using FlatList of ActivityFeedCard components
Screen background bg-brand-cloud; header is GradientHeader so navy gradient anchors top
Each card: bg-brand-mist border border-brand-paper rounded-2xl p-4 mb-4. Title font-lora-semibold text-2xl text-brand-ink. Body font-nunito text-base text-brand-ink-muted

Per-kind layouts: promotion (image 16:9 + headline + 'View promotion' → /(tabs)/promotions/[id]); post (avatar+name, body truncated 3 lines, 'Read more' → /(tabs)/community); alert (bell icon + message → /(tabs)/notifications); tenant_signup (text + 'View directory' → /(tabs)/directory/[id]); business_update (text + linked CTA); announcement (landlord text, no CTA)
Pull-to-refresh re-fetches
Empty state: friendly icon + 'No activity yet — check back soon.'
Loading state: 3 skeleton cards using existing LoadingScreen patterns
Each card min-height 88pt; tap target ≥ 44pt
cd unit && npx tsc --noEmit and npm run brand-lint pass
Maestro E2E: home loads → ≥1 card visible → tap promotion card → lands on detail
Verify in iOS simulator: 60fps scroll, no dark-on-dark issues

userStories.title: 'My Property' / 'Nearby' segmented control on Home Feed
userStories.description: As a tenant in a clustered property (e.g. Daytona), I want to switch the Home Feed between activity at my property only and activity across nearby properties (≤ 2 mi).
acceptanceCriteria: Add SegmentedControl at top of Home Feed: 'My Property' (default) / 'Nearby (≤ 2 mi)'
Nearby selection re-queries with propertyIds = [origin, ...nearbyIds] from useNearbyPropertyIds hook
If origin has zero neighbors within 2 mi, Nearby option is disabled with helper text 'No nearby properties.'
Selection persists for the session (React state only; resets on cold start)
cd unit && npx tsc --noEmit and npm run brand-lint pass
Verify in iOS simulator with two seeded properties within 2 mi: segment switching refreshes feed visibly

userStories.title: Admin 'Create External Promotion' screen
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to author a promotion on behalf of an external local business (e.g. Starbucks 10% off) without creating an advertiser account.
acceptanceCriteria: Add unit/app/(admin)/promotions/new-external.tsx

Form (react-hook-form + zod): business_name (required), headline (required), description (required), image upload (existing storage helper), cta_text + cta_link (required), external contact name/email/phone (optional), target property (single-select admin's properties), start_date + end_date (required)
On submit: INSERT promotions row with advertiser_id=NULL, created_by_admin_id=currentAdmin.id, review_status='approved' (admin-authored = pre-approved)
Add promotions.createExternal(payload) service function
Validation: business_name non-empty (matches CHECK constraint promotions_attribution_required)
Success toast → back to admin promotions list; new row shows 'External' badge

REALITY CHECK (verified): unit/app/(admin)/promotions/ contains ONLY [id].tsx — there is NO list/index file. Today the admin promotion list is rendered inside unit/app/(admin)/index.tsx via useAdminPromotions/useAdminRecentActivity hooks. This story SHALL create unit/app/(admin)/promotions/index.tsx as a dedicated full list with filters (Pending / Approved / All / External-only), display an External badge using existing Badge component for rows where advertiser_id IS NULL, and link from the existing dashboard tile. Do NOT remove the dashboard tile
cd unit && npx tsc --noEmit and npm run brand-lint pass
Maestro E2E: admin → admin promotions → New External → fill → save → row visible with External badge

userStories.title: Tenant 'Submit Promotion' entry from Profile
userStories.description: As a tenant business owner, I want to submit a promotion for my own business directly from my Profile tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Promote My Business' CTA card on unit/app/(tabs)/profile.tsx (visible only to tenants whose profile has ≥1 linked business)
Tap → routes to unit/app/(tabs)/promotions/create.tsx (extend if needed to collect headline/description/dates/image/CTA)

On submit: INSERT promotions row with advertiser_id=currentTenantBusinessId, review_status='draft', payment_status='unpaid'. SCHEMA REALITY (verified): payment_status enum is 'unpaid'|'paid'|'repayment_required'|'refunded' (NO 'pending' value); review_status enum allows 'draft'|'pending'|'approved'|'revision_requested'|'rejected'|'expired'|'suspended'. Use 'draft' until payment, then portal webhook flips to 'pending' for admin review.

On submit ALSO INSERT promotion_status_events audit row { from_review_status: null, to_review_status: 'draft', actor_user_id: currentUser.id, actor_type: 'advertiser' } — mirrors existing pattern in portal/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts
Post-submit screen shows 'Pending payment' state with 'Pay Now' button (Stripe wire-up lands in US-014)
Abandoning before payment leaves the row in payment_status='unpaid' AND review_status='draft' (NOT visible publicly — existing tenant RLS filters review_status='approved')
cd unit && npx tsc --noEmit and npm run brand-lint pass
Maestro E2E: tenant → Profile → 'Promote My Business' → fill → submit → Pending Payment screen

userStories.title: Admin Promotion Pricing configuration
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to set the price tenants pay for promotions (per duration / placement), editable from the admin section without a code change.
acceptanceCriteria: Add migration 20260502000002_promotion_price_tiers.sql creating table promotion_price_tiers with: id, name, duration_days, is_featured, price_cents (USD cents), currency (default 'usd'), is_active, created_at, updated_at
RLS: tenants SELECT where is_active=true; admins FULL ACCESS via is_landlord()
Seed two starter tiers: ('7-day Standard', 7, false, 2500), ('30-day Featured', 30, true, 9900)
Add unit/app/(admin)/pricing.tsx — admin-only screen listing tiers with inline edit (price, name, active) + 'Add Tier' CTA
Add unit/services/promotionPricing.ts (listTiers, upsertTier, deactivateTier)
Wire 'Pricing' entry into admin nav
cd unit && npx tsc --noEmit and npm run brand-lint pass
Verify in iOS simulator: admin can edit a tier price and persistence works

userStories.title: Edge Function — create-promotion-checkout-session
userStories.description: As a tenant, I want a Stripe Checkout session created server-side when I tap 'Pay Now' on my promotion. Mirrors existing portal advertiser flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Supabase Edge Function unit/supabase/functions/create-promotion-checkout-session/index.ts (Deno)
Input POST JSON: { promotionId, priceTierId }

Reads calling user from Authorization JWT; verifies promotion belongs to user (advertiser_id == userId); looks up price tier; creates Stripe Checkout Session in 'payment' mode; INSERTs promotion_payment_attempts row with status='pending', stripe_checkout_session_id=session.id, price_tier_id=priceTierId (per migration 20260502000003); returns { url, sessionId }

Stripe customer handling: tenants do NOT have stripe_customer_id (only advertiser_profiles does — verified). For tenant flows, pass customer_email to the session (Stripe creates customer on the fly). Do NOT add stripe_customer_id to profiles table in this milestone (out of scope)
Metadata: { promotionId, priceTierId, userId, source: 'mobile_tenant' } — existing portal webhook only needs promotionId
Success URL: unit://promotions/{promotionId}?status=success. Cancel URL: unit://promotions/{promotionId}?status=cancel (mobile uses expo-linking unit:// scheme — already wired up)
Reads STRIPE_SECRET_KEY from Supabase secrets via Deno.env.get('STRIPE_SECRET_KEY') — no hard-coding
Use https://esm.sh/stripe@<version> import per existing edge-function convention (see unit/supabase/functions/expire-promotions/index.ts)
Deployed to dev Supabase; smoke test via curl returns Stripe Checkout URL

userStories.title: Reuse existing portal Stripe webhook for mobile-tenant checkouts
userStories.description: Existing portal webhook at portal/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts ALREADY handles checkout.session.completed for promotions rows by reading session.metadata.promotionId, flipping payment_status='paid' + review_status='pending', inserting promotion_status_events, and push-notifying admins. No new Edge Function is needed — mobile-tenant checkouts will reuse this same webhook by inserting compatible metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Confirm portal webhook is deployed and registered in Stripe dashboard. Idempotency table stripe_webhook_events already exists per migration 20260412000001_m4_monetization.sql
Verify portal webhook handler does NOT special-case advertiser-vs-tenant (only reads metadata.promotionId) — confirmed at portal/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts lines 30-73 during PRD research
Add migration 20260502000003_promotion_payment_attempts_price_tier.sql adding price_tier_id uuid column to promotion_payment_attempts so mobile checkout can record which tier was used
If portal webhook needs the priceTierId field to persist into promotion_payment_attempts, add a small update there (otherwise the portal webhook is unchanged)

On checkout.session.expired or payment_intent.payment_failed: portal webhook should NOT change promotions.payment_status (no 'failed' value in enum). Instead update promotion_payment_attempts.status='failed' for audit. Verify current behavior; if missing, add it
Document in unit/docs/deployment-runbook.md that mobile-tenant checkout reuses portal webhook — no separate Stripe endpoint required
NEGATIVE acceptance: do NOT create unit/supabase/functions/stripe-promotion-webhook/
Smoke test: from mobile, complete a Stripe test card → portal webhook fires → promotions.payment_status='paid', review_status='pending', status_events row inserted, admin push fires
userStories.notes: REPLACES the original 'new mobile webhook' story. The portal webhook is sufficient — verified during deep PRD review.

userStories.title: Wire tenant promotion checkout flow on mobile
userStories.description: As a tenant, when I tap 'Pay Now', I want Stripe Checkout to open in-app browser, complete payment, and route me back to 'Pending review'.
acceptanceCriteria: Render tier picker on post-submit screen (useQuery against promotionPricing.listTiers) + 'Pay Now' button
'Pay Now' invokes Edge Function create-promotion-checkout-session via supabase.functions.invoke({promotionId, priceTierId})
Open returned url with expo-web-browser openAuthSessionAsync (in-app browser)

On dismissal: poll promotions.payment_status once. If 'paid' (existing portal webhook flipped it via metadata.promotionId) → 'Awaiting Review' screen. If still 'unpaid' → toast 'Payment was not completed.' SCHEMA REALITY: payment_status enum has NO 'pending' or 'failed' values; the only two outcomes the client cares about are 'unpaid' (no payment yet) and 'paid' (webhook fired)
Add expo-web-browser to dependencies if absent
cd unit && npx tsc --noEmit and npm run brand-lint pass
Maestro E2E with Stripe test card 4242 4242 4242 4242: tenant submits → pays → Awaiting Review; admin sees row with payment_status=paid AND review_status=pending

userStories.title: TypeScript types and read-only service for promotion_leads (V2 scaffold)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a thin client-side service for promotion_leads so the V2 Kanban sales workflow isn't blocked. NO UI per migration comment ('table is intentionally unused at rest until V2').
acceptanceCriteria: Add unit/services/promotionLeads.ts exporting: listLeads(filters?), createLead(input), updateLead(id, patch), convertLeadToPromotion(leadId, promotionId) — all admin-only
No screens added
Types exported as named exports
cd unit && npx tsc --noEmit and npm test pass

userStories.title: Update brand-lint to permit new light tokens & enforce surface rules
userStories.description: Existing brand-lint must permit bg-brand-cloud, bg-brand-mist, border-brand-paper, text-brand-ink, text-brand-ink-muted while continuing to block raw tokens (bg-white, bg-gray-*, text-black).
acceptanceCriteria: REALITY CHECK (verified): brand-lint is an INLINE bash one-liner in unit/package.json scripts.brand-lint. It is a DENY-list (greps for banned patterns; success = no matches). The 5 new tokens (bg-brand-cloud, bg-brand-mist, etc) DO NOT match any banned regex, so they pass the existing rules WITHOUT modification.

NEW additive rule: extend the inline regex chain in package.json so any single .tsx file containing BOTH bg-brand-mist AND text-brand-gray fails the lint (text-brand-gray on white fails AA contrast). Implementation hint: chain a `! grep -lE 'bg-brand-mist' app components | xargs grep -lE 'text-brand-gray'` clause

NEW additive rule: any file using bg-brand-cloud as a screen root MUST also import GradientHeader. Implementation hint: chain a check that files containing bg-brand-cloud also match `from '@/components/ui/GradientHeader'`
Run brand-lint on entire unit/ — zero new violations
cd unit && npm run brand-lint exits 0
userStories.notes: May need to ship together with US-001 in a single iteration so the new tokens don't fail brand-lint when first added.

dark variant: existing styling (bg-brand-navy-light etc) — pixel-identical to today
light variant: bg-brand-mist border border-brand-paper. Text inside defaults to brand-ink
Update typed props interface; export variant union
Add usage comment in file header
No existing caller is changed — all default to 'dark'

userStories.title: Dark-on-dark audit — fix contrast violations across 5 existing tabs
userStories.description: Every screen has legible text and clear card boundaries. No dark-text-on-dark-card or dark-card-on-dark-bg situations.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit each: directory.tsx, directory/[id].tsx, community.tsx, community/create.tsx, promotions.tsx, promotions/[id].tsx, promotions/create.tsx, notifications.tsx, profile.tsx, profile/edit.tsx

For each violation, apply ONE of: swap bg-brand-navy card to bg-brand-navy-light; add border border-brand-blue/40; bump brand-steel text to brand-gray on brand-navy-light (CLAUDE.md: brand-steel on brand-navy-light = 4.48:1, fails AA)
Do NOT introduce light tokens (brand-mist etc) into existing screens — that is a v2 sweep
Run accesslint-contrast-checker skill on every changed file; all pairs ≥ 4.5:1
cd unit && npm run brand-lint passes
Document before/after screenshots in progress log
